revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:阿豆良神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Azura Shrine | native_name = 阿豆良神社 | image = Azura Jinja-Shrine 20150724.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Azura Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.284|136.823917}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Azura Shrine'''|阿豆良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11465292|ltq=Q11465292|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 49 ** P155: {{ill|Q11436767|ltq=Q11436767|qid=Q11436767|1=Ōke Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Tagata Shrine|lt=Tagata Shrine|en|Tagata Shrine|fr|Tagata-jinja|ja|田縣神社|min|Kuil Tagata|simple|Tagata Shrine|zh|田縣神社|qid=Q60581|1=Tagata Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q270810|ltq=Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Niwa District|lt=Niwa district|ceb|Niwa-gun|en|Niwa District|es|Distrito de Niwa (Aichi)|fa|شهرستان نیوا، آیچی|fr|District de Niwa|id|Distrik Niwa, Aichi|it|Distretto di Niwa|ja|丹羽郡|ko|니와군|pl|Powiat Niwa|ru|Нива (уезд)|sr|Област Нива|tl|Distrito ng Niwa, Aichi|uk|Повіт Ніва|vi|Niwa, Aichi|zh|丹羽郡|qid=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.284|136.823917}} == image (P18) == * Azura Jinja-Shrine 20150724.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181389 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Japan|lt=Japan|ab|Иапониа|ace|Jeupang|ady|Японие|af|Japan|als|Japan|alt|Јопон|ami|Ripon|am|ጃፓን|ang|Iapan|anp|जापान|an|Chapón|arc|ܝܦܢ|ar|اليابان|arnews|تصنيف:اليابان|arquote|اليابان|ary|الجاپون|arz|اليابان|ast|Xapón|as|জাপান|atj|Japan|avk|Nipona|av|Япония|awa|जापान|ay|Nihun|azb|ژاپون|az|Yaponiya|azquote|Yaponiya|ban|Jepang|bar|Japan|bat_smg|Japuonėjė|ba|Япония|bbc|Japang|bcl|Hapon|bdr|Gipun|be_x_old|Японія|be|Японія|bew|Jepang|bg|Япония|bgnews|Категория:Япония|bh|जापान|bi|Japan|bjn|Japang|blk|ဂျပန်ခမ်းထီ|bm|Japan|bn|জাপান|bnquote|জাপান|bnvoyage|জাপান|bo|ཉི་ཧོང་།|bpy|জাপান|br|Japan|bs|Japan|bsnews|Kategorija:Japan|btm|Jepang|bug|ᨍᨛᨄ|bxr|Япон Улас|ca|Japó|canews|Categoria:Japó|caquote|Japó|cbk_zam|Japón|cdo|Nĭk-buōng|ceb|Hapon|ce|Ипнойчоь|chr|ᏣᏩᏂᏏ|ch|Chapan|chy|Japan|ckb|ژاپۆن|co|Giappone|crh|Japon|csb|Japòńskô|cs|Japonsko|csnews|Kategorie:Japonsko|csquote|Japonsko|csvoyage|Japonsko|cu|Ꙗпѡнїꙗ|cv|Япони|cy|Japan|dag|Japan|da|Japan|de|Japan|denews|Kategorie:Japan|dequote|Japan|devoyage|Japan|din|Japan|diq|Japonya|dsb|Japańska|dtp|Jepun|dty|जापान|dv|ޖަޕާނު|dz|ཇ་པཱན|ee|Japan|el|Ιαπωνία|elnews|Κατηγορία:Ιαπωνία|elquote|Ιαπωνία|elvoyage|Ιαπωνία|eml|Giapòṅ|en|Japan|ennews|Category:Japan|enquote|Japan|envoyage|Japan|eo|Japanio|eonews|Kategorio:Japanujo|eoquote|Japanio|eovoyage|Japanio|es|Japón|esnews|Categoría:Japón|esquote|Japón|esvoyage|Japón|et|Jaapan|etquote|Jaapan|eu|Japonia|ext|Japón|fa|ژاپن|fanews|رده:ژاپن|faquote|ژاپن|favoyage|ژاپن|ff|Japan|fiu_vro|Jaapan|fi|Japani|finews|Luokka:Japani|fivoyage|Japani|fj|Japani|fo|Japan|frp|Japon|frr|Jaapan|fr|Japon|frnews|Catégorie:Japon|frquote|Japon|frvoyage|Japon|fur|Gjapon|fy|Japan|gag|Yaponiya|gan|日本|ga|An Dineʼé Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2180005009736 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市あずら1-7-19', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:阿豆良神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11657682}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 01:53 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Azura Shrine | Image = [[File:Azura Jinja-Shrine 20150724.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall | Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Azura|Q126540297|qid=Q126540297|1=Azura}}1-7-19 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amanomikatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|qid=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank|Q11071121|qid=Q11071121|1=Junior First Rank}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
{{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Other names = Agura-na-shin
Agura Daimyōjin
Azura-na-shin | Founded = (Trad.) 57th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Azura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Azura Shrine''' (あずらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' as “Azura Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|qid=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}. It is an ancient shrine with records remaining in the ''{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|qid=Q135598023|1=Owari no Kuni Fudoki}}''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amanomikatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (also written as Ama-Mikatsuhime) *: A deity of {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo mythology|1=Izumo Province}} * {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|qid=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the 57th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=28 CE|Q24816|qid=Q24816|1=AD 28}}). Its original location is said to have been about 200 meters north of the present site. About 1 km north of the current location is a ritual site from the {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|qid=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} to the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|qid=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, known as the Mamizuka Site, which is also considered a former location. It is presumed that the shrine was relocated to its present site during reconstruction in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|qid=Q6744|1=1587}} (15th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}). The ''Owari no Kuni Fudoki'', compiled in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, contains a passage concerning Azura Village in Niwa District. According to it, a {{ill|lt=deity|Q178885|qid=Q178885|1=Deity}} calling herself Amanomikatsuhime appeared in a {{ill|lt=dream|Q36348|qid=Q36348|1=Dream}} of the {{ill|lt=empress|Q7723211|qid=Q7723211|1=Empress consort}} who was worried because {{ill|lt=Homutsuwake-no-Mikoto|Q11631423|qid=Q11631423|1=Homutsuwake no Mikoto}} could not speak even at the age of seven. The deity said, “If you assign someone to worship me, Homutsuwake-no-Mikoto will be able to speak and his lifespan will be extended.” When the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|qid=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}} ordered Tateoka no Kimi to search for and divine the whereabouts of the deity, Tateoka no Kimi broke off a branch of sakaki, made a vine (kazura), and said, “Wherever this vine falls, the deity must surely reside.” The vine flew away and fell at this spot, and a shrine was built here, believing the deity to be present. Although the credibility of these traditions is unclear, it can be said with certainty that the shrine had already been founded by the first half of the {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|qid=Q8086|1=8th century}}. The ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', compiled in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian|1=Heian period}}, lists “Niwa District … Azura Shrine.” A contemporary text, the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=UNKNOWN}}'', records it as “Niwa District … Agura,” suggesting that the name was originally written as “Azura” and later came to be written as “Agura” under the influence of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Two-Character Favorable Name Edict|1=UNKNOWN}}. Regarding the {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, various manuscripts of the ''{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Naijinmyōchō|Q11041830|qid=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}'' record it as “Junior First Rank, Agura-na-shin.” In {{ill|lt=1564|Q6632|qid=Q6632|1=1564}} (7th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku era|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}), the shrine was destroyed by fire due to warfare, but it was rebuilt in 1587 (Tenshō 15). During the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|qid=Q184963}}, it was known as '''Agura Daimyōjin'''. According to the ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|qid=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}'', compiled in the early Edo period, it was at that time under the control of Hōshō-ji (also called Hōchō-ji), with shrine land of 4 tan 3 se 1 bu and shrine fields totaling 6 se. Listed subsidiary shrines within the precincts include “Ichinomiya, Ninomiya, Sannomiya, Gongen, Hakusan, Tenjin, Hachiman, Jingūji.” The nearby Hōshō-ji served as the shrine’s {{ill|lt=jingū-ji (shrine temple)|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} until the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, and is said to have been founded in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Kōnin era|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|qid=Q30360|1=813}}). In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Azura Shrine and designated a gōsha. === Access === * About a 10-minute walk from the “Sen’i Danchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|qid=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}} ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|qid=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|qid=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} or {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|qid=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|qid=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|qid=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} ({{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal#Owari-Ichinomiya Station Front Bus Stop|Q11415757|qid=Q11415757|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station bus stop|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station bus stop}}), take the Meigi Line bound for “Kokonokaichi” {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Azura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Azura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]
